title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard protocol that enables AI applications and agents to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services. The protocol defines client-server architecture using JSON-RPC communication over STDIO or HTTP transports, allowing AI agents to access standardized integrations regardless of the underlying agent framework.
writeup: This video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard for connecting AI applications and agents to external tools, APIs, databases, and services. It covers the client-server architecture, the use of JSON-RPC for communication, and the STDIO and HTTP transport options. The key takeaway is that MCP standardizes integrations independent of the underlying agent framework, so agents can access external resources through a common interface.
